content.full_text:
June 3, 1976

Ms. Myra Schimke, Treasurer
Town & Country Democratic Club
3518 Ronk Way
Sacramento, California 95821

Dear Ms. Schimke:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on
May 28, 1976.

You stated the following facts: (1) The Town & Country Democratic
Club obtains its revenue from membership dues and fundraisers;
(2) The primary purpose of the Club is not to influence the actions
of voters but rather to sponsor social events, to hold meetings to
discuss political issues and to send out a monthly newsletter which
is a meeting notice and contains information regarding local, state
and federal legislation; (3) The Club's practice in prior years has
been to use a portion of its revenue to make political contributions;
(4) In 1976, the Club has contributed $170 for political purposes.

You asked whether the Club has an obligation at this point to file
campaign disclosure statements. I advised that since the Club does
not have a separate political fund which has collected $500 or more
this year; the primary purpose of the Club is not political; and
even though a portion of the Club's revenue is earmarked for
political purposes, the Club has only contributed $170 this year,
the Club has no filing obligations at the present time. If the
Club's contributions and expenditures for political purposes total
$500 or more this year, the Club will be required to report such
receipts and expenditures.

If you have further questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,


NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak

cc: Secretary of State's Office    bcc: Jeanne-A✓

sections.question: Whether the Club has an obligation at this point to file campaign disclosure statements.
sections.conclusion: Since the Club does not have a separate political fund which has collected $500 or more this year; the primary purpose of the Club is not political; and even though a portion of the Club's revenue is earmarked for political purposes, the Club has only contributed $170 this year, the Club has no filing obligations at the present time. If the Club's contributions and expenditures for political purposes total $500 or more this year, the Club will be required to report such receipts and expenditures.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Town & Country Democratic Club have a filing obligation to report campaign contributions and expenditures when contributions total $170 in 1976, given that the club's primary purpose is social and educational rather than political?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Club has no filing obligations at the current time because its contributions ($170) are below the $500 threshold, its primary purpose is not political, and it has no separate political fund that has collected $500 or more. However, if contributions and expenditures reach $500 or more during the year, filing requirements will be triggered.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Confirmation letter of prior telephone advice from May 28, 1976. Clear facts, question, and conclusion sections present. No withdrawal or declination.

embedding.summary: FPPC advisor confirms telephone advice to the Town & Country Democratic Club treasurer regarding filing obligations for political contributions. The Club is not required to file campaign disclosure statements at this time as contributions remain below the $500 threshold and the club's primary purpose is social and educational.
